# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { NextApiRequest, NextApiResponse } from 'next';

async function authenticate(req: NextApiRequest, res: NextApiResponse) {
  const authToken = req.cookies.auth;

  if (authToken) {
    // Check if the auth token is valid
    const user = await verifyAuthToken(authToken);

    if (user) {
      // If the token is valid, set the user object on the request
      req.user = user;
    } else {
      // If the token is not valid, return a 401 response
      res.status(401).end();
    }
  } else {
    // If there is no auth token, allow the request to continue
    // without setting a user object on the request
  }
}

async function verifyAuthToken(authToken: string): Promise<User | null> {
  // Implement your own logic to verify the auth token and return a User object if it is valid, or null if it is not.
  // For example, you could use a database query to fetch the user based on the auth token.
}
